title: Sour Chewy Slovakian Bacon Roast
description: A kid-friendly, keto-friendly roast that combines the tang of apple cider vinegar with the comforting chewiness of slow-cooked pork and crispy bacon lardons.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat. Add bacon lardons and cook until crisp, about 5 minutes. Remove and set aside, leaving 1 tbsp fat in the pan.
Sauté diced onion in the remaining fat until translucent, about 3 minutes. Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in smoked paprika, salt, pepper, and thyme; cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the pork shoulder to the skillet and sear each side until golden brown, about 2–3 minutes per side.
Deglaze the pan with apple cider vinegar, swirling to loosen browned bits. Add chicken broth and bring to a simmer.
Transfer the pork and liquid to a roasting pan. Distribute the diced mixed vegetables around the meat and sprinkle the bacon lardons over the top.
Cover the pan with foil and roast in the preheated oven for 3 hours, or until the internal temperature reaches 70 °C (160 °F) and the meat is tender.
Remove the foil, increase oven temperature to 200 °C (400 °F) and roast an additional 15–20 minutes to crisp the top.
Take the roast out, let it rest for 10 minutes, then slice and serve.
